blurb: Nesta is the UK's innovation agency for social good, running mission teams on early years, health/obesity and home decarbonisation.

hiring.note: Official Nesta jobs page (seed URL redirected/changed); bespoke HTML/manual check required.

publications.title: How to respond to the energy crisis
publications.byline: Andrew Sissons, Katy King, Madeleine Gabriel, Daniel Lewis, Marcus Shepheard and Juliette Caucheteux
publications.summary: Sets out a three-point plan for government to respond to an energy-price crisis by cutting oil and gas demand: a push on low-cost efficiency measures before winter, faster electrification over the summer, and locking in long-term energy-bill stability.

publications.title: Report on the Boiler Upgrade Scheme (heat pump subsidy)
publications.summary: Nesta report dated 05 May 2026 on the Boiler Upgrade Scheme, the UK government's flagship heat-pump subsidy, which since launching in April 2022 has supported almost 80,000 heat pump installations across England and Wales. (Direct report URL not isolated; listed on the reports index.)

news.title: The energy edit
news.summary: Nesta blog round-up of recent energy work, referencing the 05 May 2026 Boiler Upgrade Scheme report and a 30 April 2026 blog.

notes: nesta.org.uk/feed/ returns 404 (not WordPress) as flagged. The reports landing page (/reports/ and /report/) and sitemap.xml are JS/redirect-heavy; sitemap.xml lists ~1354 /report/ URLs but in arbitrary order without reliable per-item pub dates. The energy-crisis report page rendered fully via web_fetch with named authors and a real pub date corroborated by search (Apr 27 2026). Boiler Upgrade Scheme report date (05 May 2026) came from Nesta's own 'energy edit' blog listing; exact per-report URL not isolated, so the reports index URL is used. No legal/regulatory controversy with Nesta as a named party found.
